title: Limonium doriae
intro: Limonium doriae, commonly known as Doran's sea lavender, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Plumbaginaceae. The health benefits of Limonium doriae include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Limonium doriae include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Limonium doriae is a species of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. This family consists of approximately 35 genera and 900 species of succulent plants. Limonium doriae is a genus of about 160 species, commonly known as sea lavenders or statice. The species is native to the Macaronesian islands and the Mediterranean region. Limonium doriae is often found in coastal areas and dry scrublands.
